_notes: Keys are raw legacy codepoints (hex). Finale emits each glyph as a MacRoman-mapped Unicode char OR its PUA twin 0xF000+byte; both are listed and map identically. Maestro and Petrucci use the same Sonata-style layout - NO divergences found on any shared codepoint. smufl=null means 'ignore' or 'no engine target'.

0x9.evidence: TAB char (0x09) in one Maestro score consistently sits at a treble clef at line start; likely a font/encoding quirk. Verify.

0xF.evidence: control char 0x0F in one Maestro score shows a comma breath mark. Ignore.

0x11.evidence: control char 0x11 (Maestro, count 1). Near dotted note + quarter rest; unresolved.

0x21.evidence: '!'. Renders '>' accent above notes ('gone forth into all'). Ignore.

0x23.evidence: '#'. Sharp sign. Sonata #=sharp. Both families + twin.

0x26.evidence: '&'. Unambiguous G-clef at system starts (Soprano/Alto). Sonata convention. Both families + PUA twin identical.

0x29.evidence: ')'. Closes parenthesis around a '(natural)' chord. Ignore.

0x2A.evidence: '*'. Asterisk above staff referencing a note ('C Alternate'). Ignore.

0x2D.evidence: '-' (Petrucci). Short horizontal tenuto stroke above noteheads. Ignore.

0x2E.evidence: '.'. Small filled dot to the RIGHT of noteheads ('Lord.', 'death:'). Same glyph also draws repeat-barline dots. Both families + twin.

0x32.evidence: '2'. Stacked in 2/2,2/4,4/2 meters at measure starts. Both families + twin.

0x3F.evidence: '?'. Unambiguous F-clef with two dots at system starts. Both families + twin.

0x43.evidence: 'C'. Cut-common time (vertical stroke through C). No engine slot; could be special-cased as 2/2.

0x44.evidence: 'D'. In one Byzantine score alongside '>' accents; ambiguous (accent or mode letter). Ignore.

0x4A.symbol: flag 8th down (stem-down eighth: notehead top, flag at bottom)
0x4A.evidence: 'J'. Verified on isolated highlighted crop: notehead high, stem down, single flag curling at bottom-right. Both families + twin.

0x4E.evidence: 'N' (Petrucci). Renders a natural inside parentheses (editorial). Functions as a natural; parentheses are part of glyph.

0x4F.symbol: unknown (possibly tie/slur segment or notehead variant)
0x4F.evidence: 'O' (Petrucci, count 11, 1 pdf). Appears over sustained slurred/tied half notes; ambiguous. Left unmapped to avoid corrupting noteheads.

0x55.evidence: 'U'. Fermata arc+dot over held/final notes ('a tempo'). Ignore.

0x56.evidence: 'V'. G-clef with integral '8' beneath, used for Tenor systems ('Chanter/Priest', 'Ison'). Maestro+Petrucci agree.

0x57.symbol: notehead double-whole / breve (oval with side bars)
0x57.evidence: 'W'. Open oval flanked by vertical strokes; used on long held notes ('O Lord save', drone 'Ison'). Maestro+Petrucci agree.

0x5F.evidence: '_' (Petrucci). Long horizontal line extending a lyric ('wealth.___'). Ignore.

0x62.evidence: 'b'. Flat sign beside noteheads. Sonata b=flat. Both families + twin.

0x63.evidence: 'c'. Plain 'C' meter symbol at measure starts. Engine only supports 0xE080+digit, so no target; could be special-cased as 4/4.

0x6A.symbol: flag 8th up (stem-up eighth: notehead bottom, flag at top)
0x6A.evidence: 'j'. Verified on isolated highlighted crop (Ps.2096): notehead low, stem up, single flag curling at top-right. Both families + twin.

0x6E.evidence: 'n'. Natural sign beside noteheads. Sonata n=natural. Both families + twin.

0x70.evidence: 'p'. Bold-italic piano dynamic below staff. Not a notehead (verified). Dynamics -> ignore.

0x71.evidence: 'q'. Standalone quarter note in 'Andante q=62'. Composite quarter used only in tempo text (staff quarters use separate notehead+stem). One Maestro score.

0x72.evidence: 'r' (Petrucci, count 1). In a beamed sixteenth run; could be a beamed 16th note/32nd.

0x77.evidence: 'w'. Tight+context show stemless open ovals on cadential 'lu-ia' whole notes. Sonata convention w=whole.

0x84.evidence: control char (Maestro, count 1). In 'Intonation #1 Allegro q=150 D' header; unresolved.

0x90.evidence: control char (Maestro, count 1). Same header context; unresolved.

0x95.evidence: control char (Maestro, count 1). Same header context; unresolved.

0xA1.evidence: 'inverted-exclamation' U+00A1 (MacRoman byte 0xC1). Comma above staff at phrase ends ('Faith; thou'); confirmed on enlarged Maestro context. Ignore. High Maestro count (77). Twin 0xF0C1.

0xA7.evidence: 'section' byte 0xA4 (Maestro, count 1). In 'Andante q.=62'; possibly metronome dot/note. Unresolved.

0xB4.evidence: 'acute' (byte 0xAB). Small downward wedge above notes near mode letters. Ignore.

0xC4.evidence: 'A-dieresis' byte 0x80 (Maestro, count 1). Same header context; unresolved.

0xCC.evidence: 'I-grave' (byte 0xED). Comma breath mark ('thy word. There-by'). Ignore.

0xD3.evidence: 'O-acute' (0x00D3). Filled rectangle sitting on a staff line. Paired opposite to whole-rest 0x2211; whole/half could be swapped - verify.

0x152.evidence: 'OE' (0x0152). Classic quarter-rest squiggle on staff. Sonata OE=quarter rest. Both families + twin.

0x153.evidence: Most common glyph (M5501/P8350). Tight+context show filled oval noteheads on staff for quarter/eighth notes; identical across Maestro/Petrucci and its PUA twin.

0x2D9.evidence: SURPRISE: the DOT-ABOVE codepoint is actually the OPEN half notehead. Tight+context show open ovals with stems on 'Al-le-lu-ia' sustained notes. Confirmed both families.

0x2DA.evidence: 'ring-above' (Petrucci). Notehead bottom, stem up, DOUBLE flag at top; appears in dotted-8th+16th rhythms. Mirrors 8th flags j/J. Low count (P8).

0x2030.evidence: 'per-mille'. Eighth-rest (hooked stroke) confirmed via PUA twin 0xF0E4 and context. Both families.

0x2122.evidence: 'TM' (byte 0xAA). Quarter note in 'q=120' tempo marks. Confirmed via context.

0x2211.evidence: 'n-ary-sum'. Filled rectangle hanging from a staff line, used for full-measure rests. Distinguished from half-rest 0x00D3 by hang-below vs sit-on; could be swapped with half rest - verify.

0x221E.evidence: 'infinity' byte 0xB0 (Maestro, count 1). In 'Byzantine Tone 8 q=56 4/4' header near whole notes; unresolved.

0xF0A2.evidence: byte 0xA2 (Petrucci, count 1). Quarter note in 'q=104' tempo. Different scores encode the tempo note under different bytes; all render as quarter.

0xF0DE.evidence: byte 0xDE twin of 0xFB01; circle-plus next to 'Coda'.

0xF0F0.evidence: byte 0xF0 (Petrucci). Notehead top, stem down, DOUBLE flag at bottom, in dotted-16th rhythms. Low count (P4). Direction read from tight crop.

0xF0FA.evidence: Renders identically to 0x2D9 (byte 0xFA): open half notehead.

0xFB01.evidence: 'fi'-ligature U+FB01 (MacRoman byte 0xDE). Circle-plus Coda navigation sign next to 'Coda' text. Ignore. Twin 0xF0DE.
